title: The ubiquitous use of self and meta references
display_title: The ubiquitous use of self and meta references
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] The ubiquitous use of self- and meta-references             Quote Post by CATO » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:39 pm Anno is no stranger to these devices and he explicitly used them in End of Evangelion and EoTV to criticize otaku culture. However, I do not remember a particular instance during the regular episodes in which he used them except from Jet Alone which could be considered a parody. After seeing Q, I think he is using self-reference everywhere. Things that other people consider as easter eggs or proof of the prequel/sequel theory, seem only to be self-references without a necessary connection to the story: the giant chalk outline, the coffins in the moon, the old Nerv logo. Other things seem to be meta-references: the opening scene in 1.0, Fuyutsuki's "31 turns" and the passive aggressive titles (can (not) redo/advance). I would argue that Kaworu is a character exhibiting some knowledge that breaks the fourth wall based on his constant remarks about meeting Shinji in the past and in the possible future. I can only conclude that Anno made films with deliberate references to his previous work and that he is expecting his viewers to recognize these. Different from the remarks of people saying he is casting his original fanbase aside, I think he is catering to them. The question is why? The plot in the films does not require this. So, the answer must be in his intentions. Will Rebuild ultimately be a postmodernist work designed to portray/comment/critique on Eva fans in particular? Anno's original plot to subvert otaku culture had limited results, so perhaps the only meaningful way to make an impact this time is to target Evangelion fans, who ironically can sometimes be worse. Please be patient with my rant and tell me what you think.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:56 pm Personally i try to hold back my judgments on possible implications of NME on the Fanbase, Anime industry, on the Eva franchise itself untill its completed. 4.0 has to much weight for the whole project to make assumption this early. With 3.0 Anno showed that he is not afraid to try something new, wether he was something in mind with it, is just to hard to say. Generally though, re-using imagery and self reference materiel isnt something new for the Evangelion franchise. Infact, it appeared very often during NGE+EoE. Those had probably a different meaning to it. I dont want to cut this out, but i think going "meta" might be a bit overanalyzing at this point, considering Anno is in a different state of mind with this project, at least to the official state.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] The ubiquitous use of self- and meta-referenc             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Apr 28, 2013 6:12 pm CATO wrote: Will Rebuild ultimately be a postmodernist work designed to portray/comment/critique on Eva fans in particular?

[]:             Quote Post by CATO » Mon Apr 29, 2013 9:18 pm I also personally think 3.33 might be a jab at how a lot of modern anime have some version of "Resetting the world/time" as a plot device to undo bad and negative consequences

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Apr 29, 2013 9:25 pm It all seemed to be self-references to me so far as well, rather than evidence to a sequel theory. (FINAL can prove me wrong, though. We'll see.) Seeing as how Anno's been using numeric titles in New Eva just like he did Kare Kano, as well as music from both that and Nadia, suggests that it's all self-reference. In fact, the double-entry system in Unit 13 and the 14-year time skip might have been the only way to incorporate Gunbuster references into the plot without using non-Shiro SAGISU music from the OVA. (That, and it makes the movies crazier.)
